summary: Hotz argues that current societal elites and power structures are fundamentally broken, unsustainable, and built on fake money and mass indoctrination. Using his 2020 rap lyrics as a framework, he critiques the hollowness of modern institutions, from education to politics, arguing they prioritize control over people rather than genuine progress. He sees technology and the internet as forces that will eventually expose and replace these corrupt systems. The piece ends with hope that a new generation of truth-seekers will rise to build something better, fighting for power over nature rather than power over people.
key_points: Current elites maintain power through fake money (printed trillions), indoctrination disguised as education, and atomization of people into consumers
Attacks on meritocracy come from people with no skills, echoing how Socrates was killed by mediocre losers
The system is propped up by increasingly hollow efforts, like a decrepit amusement park demanding you buy tickets for broken rides
Corporate and political support for certain movements reveals their co-optation, unlike genuine grassroots efforts like Occupy Wall Street
The internet and technology will ultimately expose and dismantle these fake structures, enabling a world focused on conquering nature rather than controlling people
money_quotes: I was not socialized by society. I did not graduate college, I did not do well in school. I am not a part of the existing power structure, and I don't have to play by their rules.
You can't make fake money and you can't atomize and dehumanize people to better sell them landfill junk.
Socrates was a dirty hippie who was killed by a bunch of losers.
Today, that sentiment lives on whenever people with no skills of their own attack concepts like 'meritocracy'.
The computers are coming, and nature will not stand for any of this bullshit!
They printed 7 trillion fake dollars to try to bribe people into keeping to support their fake system!
Education is simply indoctrination by another name.
I dream of a world where we fight for power over nature instead of power over people.
key_insight: Modern institutions are hollow facades maintained by fake money and mass indoctrination, but technology and a rising generation of outsiders will eventually replace control over people with mastery over nature.
